title: molinia caerulea subsp. altissima
intro: Molinia caerulea subsp. altissima, commonly known as tall purple moor-grass, is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall, slender stems and purple inflorescences. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be utilized to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Molinia caerulea subsp. altissima include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
